meaning: Two special letters (Ta Ha) that begin this surah
a gradual sending down (as the object being acted upon)
The Most Merciful (one of Allah's names)
all of you stay! (command to group)
surely perhaps I (will bring you from it)
I will bring to you all (addressing Musa's family)
with a flame (referring to an unspecified amount from the fire)
or (connecting two possibilities: bringing an ember OR finding guidance)
I find (referring to finding guidance at the fire)
he came to it/her (referring to the fire mentioned earlier)
so take off! (command to Moses to remove his shoes)
in the valley (location where Allah is speaking to Moses)
Tuwa (name of sacred valley mentioned in the Quran)
so that she/it is given what is due
that (feminine demonstrative pronoun pointing to something far)
needs and purposes (as the main subject)
throw it! (command to Moses about his staff)
and then he threw it (referring to his staff)
and suddenly/and then (introduces the staff becoming a snake)
take it! (command to one person to take the staff)
O my Lord! (calling upon the Lord)
a knot (that Moses wants untied from his tongue)
your request (that was given to you)
and so that you may be created
and then you say/speak (to your sister)
he cares for him (referring to baby Moses)
so We returned you (to your mother)
the distress (that you were saved from)
and we tested you through many trials
with much testing (describing how Allah tested Moses)
Madyan (name of an ancient city where Prophet Musa stayed)
you both weaken (addressing Moses and Aaron)
and say! / so say! (command to two people)
very gently/softly (as the manner of speaking)
Oh our Lord! (calling out to Him)
I hear/listen (present tense, in this ayah showing Allah speaking)
so you two go to him! (command to Moses and Aaron about Pharaoh)
and the peace/safety (greeting in response to following guidance)
your (both of you) lord (addressing Moses and Aaron)
its creation (what was given to everything)
and so we brought out (after sending down water)
We created you all (referring to all humans)
all of them (referring to Our signs)
so that you make us leave
so most surely we will come to you
a place (that we should make neutral between us)
morning time (when Moses agreed to meet)
and then he turned himself away
to/for them (who Musa is speaking to)
woe to you! (warning to the people Musa is addressing)
and he will destroy you all completely
between them (referring to the magicians who are discussing their plan)
those two make you all exit
your (all of you) land (that Pharaoh's people are speaking about to each other)
in a row/line (describing how they should come)
who (asking about the person who will throw first)
all of you throw! (command to a group)
their magic (referring to the magicians' tricks that made their ropes appear to move)
and so they were thrown down
we believed (said by the magicians to Pharaoh)
surely (he is) your leader (addressing Pharaoh's magicians)
so I will most certainly cut off
And surely I will crucify you (pl)
upon/over (something that will be mentioned after)
this (when pointing to something feminine near)
upon him (referring to what they were forced to do)
a believer (as someone who comes to Allah in the ayah)
so those (connecting to what was mentioned before)
for them (referring to those who come as believers having done righteous deeds)
from (showing that the rivers flow from beneath the gardens)
the rivers (which flow under the gardens)
reward (as the main subject of the sentence)
travel at night! (command to Prophet Moses)
and then he made them follow
and then it covered/overwhelmed them (referring to the sea covering Pharaoh's army)
he covered/overwhelmed them (referring to Pharaoh's forces being overwhelmed by the sea)
and we made a mutual promise to you all
one who goes to the side
the manna (the special food Allah gave as a gift)
truly one who constantly covers up faults
he returned back (from wrong to right)
they/them (referring to the people who went astray in this ayah)
O my people! (Moses speaking to his people)
your Lord (addressing the Children of Israel)
has it become long? (questioning the long wait)
by/with our power (explaining how they did something)
to/for them (referring to the people who made the calf)
and so they said (following the previous action in the ayah)
your god (referring to the golden calf they made)
Then do they not...? (introduces a rhetorical question)
O my people! (calling out to them)
so all of you follow me! (plural command)
to us (referring to the people speaking to Moses)
with what (referring to what they did not see)
you will fail to fulfill it
who/which/that (referring to your god that you remained devoted to)
surely we will definitely burn it intensely
we will most certainly scatter it
like that, in this way (referring to what was mentioned before)
a burden (as something being carried on the Day of Judgment)
if (introducing a condition in this ayah)
except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
and then he leaves/abandons it (referring to the earth)
completely leveled and smooth (describing how Allah will leave the earth in this ayah)
in it (referring to the earth/ground mentioned earlier)
and it/she became humble and still
a soft sound/whisper (as the thing being heard)
their hands (what is in front of them)
the faces (as the doer of humbling)
any unfair decrease (of what is due to them)
my Lord! (addressing Allah directly with emphasis)
increase for me! (command to increase knowledge for me)
to Adam (showing who they should prostrate to)
he will surely expel you both
and thus you will struggle/suffer (consequence of being expelled from Paradise)
do you want to know if
and then they both ate (referring to Adam and his wife)
their (dual) private parts (referring to Adam and Eve)
they both patch/cover (referring to Adam and Eve)
so he turned back in repentance
you two go down! (command for two people)
all together (describing how they should go down)
he loses/is losing the right way
the resurrection (specifically the Day of Judgment, being described in this ayah)
O my Lord! (being called out to)
why (asking about the reason he was raised blind)
like that (referring to how the person ignored Allah's signs)
so you forgot/neglected it (referring to Allah's signs)
he guides (in a conditional or command context)
and if not for (connects to previous statement)
so be patient! (in response to what they say)
with it/him (referring to what was given to others)
from them (referring to the worldly pleasures mentioned in the ayah)
so that we may test them (referring to those who are attracted to worldly pleasures mentioned in the ayah)
in it (referring to what We have provided as enjoyment)
and even if/although (connects this hypothetical situation to what came before)
one who waits and watches intensively
so all of you wait watchfully (command to multiple people)
the very straight/proper one (referring to the path mentioned before)
